message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes (bv flag format update)

- Update bv integration to use -robot-* flags (single dash) instead of
  --robot-* (double dash) to match updated bv CLI convention
- Add matchAssignmentPattern tests for glob pattern matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1g1v","title":"Task: Create assign.go command","description":"## Purpose
Implement ntm assign command for intelligent task-to-agent assignment.

## Command
ntm assign myproject [--auto] [--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency]

## Command Flow
1. Query BV: bv --robot-triage for prioritized work
2. Query NTM: Get idle agents and their types/context usage
3. Run assignment algorithm
4. Display recommendations or execute

## Output
Task Assignment Recommendations for myproject
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Ready Work (from BV):
  bd-42 [P1] Refactor authentication module
  bd-45 [P2] Add unit tests for API

Available Agents:
  cc_1 (Claude/Opus) - 34% context, idle 5m
  cod_1 (Codex) - 45% context, idle 8m

Recommended Assignments:
  cc_1 -> bd-42 (refactoring=0.95)
  cod_1 -> bd-47 (bug fix=0.90)

Execute all? [y/N]

## Acceptance Criteria
- Command shows recommendations
- --auto executes without confirmation
- Strategy flag affects assignment
- Graceful when BV unavailable

## Technical Notes
Create internal/cli/assign.go
Integrate with bv --robot-triage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:04.350484504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:43.803824037Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:43.803747402Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm assign command with text UI, JSON output, multiple strategies (balanced/speed/quality/dependency), interactive confirmation, and auto mode","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g1v","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g2by","title":"Task: Implement finding-to-bead export command","description":"## Implementation
Add `ntm ensemble export-findings` command:

1. Load findings from last run or `--run-id`.
2. Interactive selector for findings.
3. For each selected finding, call `br create` with:
   - Title from finding thesis
   - Body includes provenance + evidence
   - Priority mapped from severity
4. Support `--all` and `--dry-run` preview.

## Logging
- Log selected finding IDs + mapped priority
- Log `br create` results (bead IDs)

## Tests
- Unit: severity→priority mapping
- Integration: stub br runner
- E2E: dry-run + real create in temp .beads project

## Dependencies

- Provenance tracking (bd-364w3)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:18:03.942744314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:49:20.011924236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:49:20.011709852Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g2by","depends_on_id":"bd-37ge8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1g5t8","title":"Task: Extend balanced strategy with assignment tracking","description":"## Overview
Balanced strategy already exists. This task extends it to use **real assignment tracking data** (from `bd-92e1`) and improves tie‑breakers without changing the public strategy name.

## Why
Current balanced behavior assumes assignment counts are populated. Without tracking, all counts are zero, so the strategy degenerates to simple capability scoring. We want true load‑aware balancing once tracking is available.

## Changes (extend, don’t replace)
- Wire assignment tracking into `assign.Agent.Assignments` (live counts per agent).
- Add optional tie‑breakers:
  1) fewer active assignments
  2) idle status
  3) least‑recent assignment timestamp
  4) best capability score
- Keep deterministic ordering for identical inputs.

## Inputs
- Assignment tracker state (`bd-92e1`).
- Agent health/availability (if available in current context).

## Outputs
- Deterministic assignment map (pane -> bead).
- Robot output includes per‑agent load snapshot and reasons (if present).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Balanced strategy still returns consistent assignments for the same inputs.
- Assignment counts reflect real state when tracking is enabled.
- Graceful fallback when tracking data is unavailable.
- Tests updated to cover load‑aware tie‑breakers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:40.168441307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660098480Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660054547Z","close_reason":"Implemented load-aware balanced strategy with tie-breakers: 1) fewer active assignments, 2) capability score, 3) least-recently assigned, 4) lower pane index for determinism. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g9wv","title":"Feature: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## Overview

Enrich the existing `ntm --robot-status` output with critical operational data that
is currently missing but essential for automated swarm management.

## Background

The current --robot-status returns basic pane information (index, agent_type, is_active)
but lacks the data needed to:
- Detect rate-limited panes
- Identify hung processes
- Know which PID to kill for restart
- Track output freshness for stuck detection

## Proposed Schema Addition

Each pane object in the status output should include:

```json
{
  "index": 2,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "pid": 12345,                    // Shell PID (already available)
  "child_pid": 12346,              // NEW: The actual agent process PID
  "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T...", // NEW: Last output timestamp
  "rate_limit_detected": false,    // NEW: Pattern match on recent output
  "process_state": "running",      // NEW: running|sleeping|zombie|dead
  "memory_mb": 450,                // NEW: Process memory usage
  "output_lines_since_last": 42    // NEW: Activity indicator
}
```

## Justification for Each Field

1. **child_pid**: Required for kill -9 when soft restart fails. Currently operators
   must run `pgrep -P <shell_pid>` manually.

2. **last_output_ts**: Enables timeout-based stuck detection without external state.
   "No output for 5 minutes" = likely stuck.

3. **rate_limit_detected**: Pattern match for "You've hit your limit" or similar.
   This is the #1 failure mode in practice. Must be detected programmatically.

4. **process_state**: From /proc/<pid>/status. Zombie processes need different handling
   than running ones.

5. **memory_mb**: Detect runaway processes consuming excessive memory.

6. **output_lines_since_last**: Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing.

## Implementation Notes

- child_pid: Use pgrep -P or parse /proc/<pid>/task
- last_output_ts: Track during capture-pane operations
- rate_limit_detected: Grep last N lines of pane output
- process_state: Read /proc/<pid>/status State field
- memory_mb: Read /proc/<pid>/status VmRSS field
- These should be gathered efficiently (single pass where possible)

## Performance Considerations

- Status is called frequently; keep it fast
- Pattern matching for rate limits should be bounded (last 50 lines)
- Process state reads are cheap (procfs)
- Consider caching with short TTL if needed

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for each field extraction
- E2E tests with actual stuck/rate-limited agents
- Performance benchmarks for large swarms (20+ panes)","notes":"Robot Status Enrichments feature is complete. All essential fields implemented: rate_limit_detected (bd-1mcvs), child_pid (bd-36dd7), last_output_ts (bd-3hxdl), process_state (bd-3ivl8), memory_mb (bd-lo0ko). The output_lines_since_last field (bd-33tuj) was deliberately skipped for performance reasons per existing comment in status_enrich.go.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:13:19.647496277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086289872Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086191316Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g9wv","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gbt5","title":"Add ntm assign --reassign for moving bead between agents","description":"## Overview
Allow users to move an assignment from one agent to another without losing tracking state.

## Use Cases
1. Agent is stuck and user wants to give bead to a faster agent
2. User realizes a bead is better suited for a different agent type
3. Load balancing - redistribute work mid-session

## Usage
```bash
# Reassign bead to a different pane
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Reassign to specific agent type (picks idle agent of that type)
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-type=codex

# Reassign with new prompt
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4 --prompt="Continue from where the previous agent left off"
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Get current assignment info
3. Update assignment status to "reassigned"
4. Create new assignment for target agent
5. Release file reservations from old agent (if any)
6. Create file reservations for new agent (if any)
7. Send prompt to new agent
8. Return reassignment result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "reassigned": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 4,
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "agent_name": "BlueLake",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
      "previous_pane": 2,
      "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
      "previous_agent_type": "claude",
      "previous_status": "working",
      "file_reservations_transferred": true
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "TARGET_BUSY",
    "message": "pane 4 already has assignment bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "current_bead": "bd-abc",
      "current_status": "working"
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- TARGET_BUSY: Target pane already has an assignment
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Target pane doesn't exist
- NO_IDLE_AGENT: No idle agent of specified type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reassign bead to specific pane
- [ ] Reassign by agent type (auto-select idle)
- [ ] Transfer file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Update assignment store correctly
- [ ] Optional custom prompt
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment state update logic
- Unit test: File reservation transfer
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Full reassignment workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:36:52.859683490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854201422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854147310Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jxy","title":"Fix list item deduplication in activity panel","description":"## Problem
The activity panel shows entries that APPEAR duplicated but may actually be different panes with similar names that become indistinguishable after truncation.

## Screenshot Evidence Analysis
Left panel shows approximately 17 rows all displaying "destructive_command_guar..." with different prefixes (★, blank).

**Key insight:** These are likely DIFFERENT panes:
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_1`
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_2`
- ...
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_17`

When truncated with end-ellipsis, they ALL become:
- `destructive_command_guar...` (INDISTINGUISHABLE)

## Root Cause: TWO Issues

### Issue A: Actual Duplicates (if present)
If the same entry truly appears multiple times, this is a data/render bug.

### Issue B: Truncation Making Different Items Look Identical (MORE LIKELY)
The TruncateRunes function cuts from the end, removing the distinguishing suffix.
**This is now fixed by bd-2qcn** which implements TruncateMiddle.

## Fix Implementation

### For Issue A: Deduplication
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) SetData(activities []Activity) {
    // Deduplicate by unique key
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    unique := make([]Activity, 0, len(activities))
    for _, a := range activities {
        key := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", a.PaneID, a.Timestamp)
        if !seen[key] {
            seen[key] = true
            unique = append(unique, a)
        }
    }
    p.list.SetItems(unique)
}
\`\`\`

### For Issue B: Smart Truncation (PRIMARY FIX)
Use the new TruncateMiddle function from bd-2qcn:
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) renderItem(item Activity) string {
    // Use middle truncation to preserve distinguishing suffix
    name := layout.TruncateMiddle(item.Name, p.nameWidth)
    // Result: "destru…_cc_1", "destru…_cc_2" (DISTINGUISHABLE!)
    return name
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No TRUE duplicates (same pane ID appears once)
- [ ] Different panes with similar names are VISUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE
- [ ] Items are properly ordered (most recent first)
- [ ] Refresh doesn't cause duplication

## Unit Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with same ID (should dedupe)
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "pane_2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    
    // Should have 2 items, not 3
    if panel.ItemCount() != 2 {
        t.Errorf("expected 2 items after dedup, got %d", panel.ItemCount())
    }
}

func TestActivityPanel_SimilarNamesDistinguishable(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    panel.SetSize(40, 20)
    
    // Add activities with similar names
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_1"},
        {ID: "2", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_2"},
        {ID: "16", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_16"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    // Each truncated name should be unique in the view
    lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, line := range lines {
        trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
        if trimmed == "" || !strings.Contains(trimmed, "destru") {
            continue
        }
        if seen[trimmed] {
            t.Errorf("duplicate line in view: %q", trimmed)
        }
        seen[trimmed] = true
    }
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Tests
1. Launch dashboard with multiple similar-named panes - verify they're distinguishable
2. Spawn new pane - verify it appears once
3. Kill pane - verify it's removed
4. Rapid refresh - verify no accumulation

## Logging
\`\`\`go
log.Debug("activity panel rendering",
    "total_items", len(activities),
    "deduped_count", len(unique),
    "duplicates_removed", len(activities) - len(unique))

// Log if similar names detected
if hasSimilarNames(unique) {
    log.Debug("similar names detected, using middle truncation")
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- **bd-2qcn** (TruncateMiddle) - PRIMARY dependency for making similar items distinguishable
- **bd-1xoe** (content height) - Ensures panel content doesn't overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:18.819688166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:04.991439382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:04:48.516792842Z","close_reason":"Fixed visually identical pane titles by implementing TruncatePaneTitle() in layout.go that preserves agent suffix (__cc_1, __gmi_2, etc.) when truncating. Panes with the same project prefix now remain distinguishable. Also cleaned up duplicate terminal package code.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qcn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3k4sh","title":"Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions","description":"# Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions

Provide templated prompts for the synthesizer agent, with strategy-specific instructions and structured rendering of mode outputs.

## Component Overview
Templates structure how collected ModeOutput data is presented to the synthesizer agent.

## Base Template
```
# Ensemble Synthesis Task

You are the SYNTHESIZER for a reasoning ensemble. Multiple agents have analyzed the problem using distinct reasoning modes. Your task is to synthesize their outputs into a coherent, actionable analysis.

## Problem Statement
{{.Problem}}

## Synthesis Strategy: {{.Strategy}}
{{.StrategyInstructions}}

## Agent Outputs
{{range .Outputs}}
### {{.ModeName}} ({{.ModeID}}{{if .ModeCode}} / {{.ModeCode}}{{end}})
{{.Content}}
---
{{end}}

## Your Task
{{.SynthesisInstructions}}
```

## Strategy-Specific Instructions
- Align with strategy definitions in bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).
- Manual/voting/argumentation strategies may bypass the synthesizer agent; template should still exist for agent-based variants when requested.
- Each strategy should define explicit focus and required output sections.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/templates.go
type SynthesisTemplate struct {
    Name     string
    Strategy SynthesisStrategy
    Template *template.Template
}

func (t *SynthesisTemplate) Render(input *SynthesisInput) (string, error)
func LoadTemplates() map[SynthesisStrategy]*SynthesisTemplate
func GetTemplate(strategy SynthesisStrategy) (*SynthesisTemplate, error)
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/templates.go

## Dependencies
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)
- Synthesis system feature (bd-2qwm8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base template renders without error
- [ ] All strategies have templates
- [ ] Templates include all mode outputs in stable order
- [ ] Errors surfaced with helpful context","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:55.160470750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462331499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462311050Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kb1","title":"Task: Implement RateLimitTracker","description":"## Purpose
Track rate limit events and learn optimal spawn/send timing.

## RateLimitTracker Struct
type RateLimitTracker struct {
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    history map[string][]RateLimitEvent // provider -> events
    delays  map[string]time.Duration    // provider -> current optimal delay
}

type RateLimitEvent struct {
    Time     time.Time
    Provider string
    Action   string // 'spawn' or 'send'
}

## Key Methods
func (t *RateLimitTracker) RecordRateLimit(provider string)
func (t *RateLimitTracker) GetOptimalDelay(provider string) time.Duration
func (t *RateLimitTracker) RecordSuccess(provider string)

## Adaptive Learning
- On rate limit: increase delay by 50%
- On 10 consecutive successes: decrease delay by 10%
- Never go below minimum (anthropic: 5s, openai: 3s)

## Default Delays
anthropic: 15s
openai: 10s
google: 8s

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tracks rate limit events
- Adjusts delays adaptively
- Persists learned delays
- Thread-safe

## Technical Notes
Store in .ntm/rate_limits.json","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3kfv0","title":"Task: Add JFP tool adapter (detect/version/health)","description":"## Overview
Implement a tools adapter for JFP (JeffreysPrompts CLI).

## Responsibilities
- Detect binary (`jfp`)
- Parse version output (`jfp --version`)
- Capabilities: list, search, show, install, export, robot_mode (JSON)
- Health check: `jfp --version` or a fast `jfp list --json` if safe

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter reports installed + version correctly
- Health check returns clear message when jfp missing or broken","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3kmf","title":"Feature: ntm send --batch (explicit sequence)","description":"## Overview
Provide an explicit, **single‑invocation** prompt batch for `ntm send` without persistent state or background tracking.

## User Story
As a user orchestrating multiple agents, I want to send a sequence of prompts from a file so that I can prepare my prompts offline and execute them in a controlled, deterministic order.

## Scope
- `ntm send <session> --batch file.txt` (one prompt per line or `---` separator)
- Sends prompts in order with **optional delay** between prompts
- Optional `--confirm-each` to gate each send on user confirmation
- Optional `--delay=5s` to add delay between prompts (rate limiting)
- Optional `--stop-on-error` to halt batch on first send failure

## Batch File Format
```
# Lines starting with # are comments
First prompt goes here
---
Second prompt goes here (can be multi-line
with continuation)
---
Third prompt
```

## Progress Display
```
Sending prompt 1/5 to cc_1... done
Sending prompt 2/5 to cc_2... done
Sending prompt 3/5 to cc_3... error: agent not responding
Batch paused. Continue? (y/n/skip)
```

## Error Handling
- On send failure: pause and prompt user (continue/skip/abort)
- With `--stop-on-error`: abort immediately on first failure
- Log all failures to stderr with context

## Agent Targeting
- Default: round-robin across all agents
- `--filter cc`: only to Claude agents
- `--agent cc_1`: to specific agent only
- `--broadcast`: same prompt to all agents simultaneously

## Non‑Goals
- No background queue/state machine
- No automatic chaining based on agent output
- No retry logic (user decides on failure)

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Basic batch send
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt

# With confirmation for each prompt
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --confirm-each

# With delay between prompts (avoid rate limits)
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --delay 5s

# Only to Claude agents
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --filter cc

# Broadcast same prompt file to all agents
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --broadcast
```

## Implementation Notes
- Parse batch file on startup, validate all prompts before sending
- Show total count before starting: "Batch contains 5 prompts"
- Track and display progress: "Sent 3/5 prompts"
- On Ctrl+C: gracefully stop, show summary of what was sent

## Tests
- Unit: batch parsing, comment handling, separator handling, empty lines
- Unit: delay timing, progress calculation
- E2E: batch send with confirmation + detailed logging
- E2E: error handling with --stop-on-error
- E2E: agent targeting with --filter and --agent

## Logging
```go
log.Printf("[E2E-SEND-BATCH] session=%s total=%d sent=%d failed=%d", session, total, sent, failed)
log.Printf("[E2E-SEND-BATCH] prompt=%d agent=%s status=%s latency_ms=%d", idx, agent, status, latency)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompts sent in deterministic order
- [ ] Safe confirmation flow works with --confirm-each
- [ ] Progress displayed during batch execution
- [ ] Error handling pauses and prompts user
- [ ] Works with --filter for agent type filtering
- [ ] Works with --agent for specific agent targeting
- [ ] Delay option properly spaces prompts
- [ ] Ctrl+C gracefully stops with summary","notes":"Rescoped from automatic chaining to explicit batch send to avoid state machine complexity.\n\n## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

Users can send next prompt when ready. Automation of prompt sequencing adds complexity without clear benefit.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kmf","depends_on_id":"bd-10ufs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
